channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: GE Vernova’s CEO on thriving through tariffs and supply chain shifts
item.description: Amid tariff whiplash and the rejuggling of global trade, GE Vernova’s CEO Scott Strazik is finding a way to stay “relentlessly optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: New iPhone 17 Pro render ditches the two-tone design for a possibly more accurate appearance
item.description: A new iPhone 17 Pro render differs from earlier ones as it doesn't show a two-tone design, but this might be more accurate.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: EU equips US-bound staff with burner phones
item.description: The European Commission has issued so-called burner phones and simple laptops to certain staffers traveling to the US to reduce the risk of espionage, according to The Financial Times.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The ‘chicken jockey’ trend is turning ‘Minecraft’ screenings into total chaos
item.description: If you’re planning to see the new Minecraft movie and haven’t heard of the viral “chicken jockey” trend wreaking havoc in theaters across the country, read on.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: CVE funding cuts reversed after security concerns raised
item.description: Editor’s note: After this story was published earlier today, CISA signed a contract extension that averts a shutdown of the MITRE CVE program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AMD, Nvidia Face Huge Charges Due to Export Restrictions
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Nvidia Corp. announced significant charges following the Trump administration's new restrictions on semiconductor exports to China.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Freely’s next upgrade will bring 16 new free channels to the streaming service – here’s the full list
item.description: Freely is the go-to for on-demand streaming and live TV viewing, now it's getting ready to bring 16 new channels to the platform.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: DHS cancels federal neurodiversity workforce contract
item.description: DHS did not provide a reason for cancelling CISA's neurodiversity workforce pilot, but it came amid the Trump administration's anti-DEI push. The post DHS cancels federal neurodiversity workforce contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Zuckerberg suggested spinning Instagram off into separate company
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg previously considered spinning off Instagram into a separate company in the face of growing antitrust concerns and competing interests with Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Underwater robots, great white sharks and glowing jellyfish: New $50 million high-tech ship arrives to unlock ocean mysteries
item.description: The 'David Packard' is named for the Silicon Valley pioneer who donated more than $1 billion to ocean research.
item.dc:creator: Paul Rogers, The Mercury News

item.title: Seth Rogen calls out Silicon Valley's Trump support in cut awards-show remarks: Report
item.description: Seth Rogen's remarks critical of President Trump and his support from tech executives at a ceremony "honoring scientists as heroes of our society" were reportedly cut from a stream of the event.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: The speed and scope of the federal government’s efforts to terminate the legal status of international students have stunned colleges and universities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Makiya Seminera And Christopher L. Keller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cato Networks augments CASB with genAI security
item.description: Cato Networks recently unveiled new generative AI capabilities in its Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that the secure access service edge (SASE) provider says will let enterprise IT organizations detect, analyze, and gain insights into the use of genAI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hello, Flipper! Google has developed a genAI model to talk to dolphins
item.description: Google has unveiled a new generative AI (genAI) model, Dolphin Gemma, which will attempt to decode how dolphins communicate with each other. Dolphins are among the most intelligent animals on the planet and communicate partly through whistles and calls.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success
item.description: Federal agencies should consider widening their focus on AI implementations beyond just generative AI. The post AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: Use APIs to understand cloud vulnerabilities, recommends the public sector solutions engineering lead for Wiz. The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: Wiz’s Chris Saunders on extending ZTA to cloud first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Windows Server 2025 domain controllers may lose connectivity after reboot, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft is warning IT administrators that their Windows Server 2025 domain controllers (DC) may lose network connectivity after they are restarted.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: More than $35,000 recovered in stolen Bitcoin scam for Ohio woman
item.description: An Ohio woman who fell victim to a cryptocurrency scam has been refunded most of the nearly $42,000 in stolen Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster
item.description: Shadi Azoum, the NAVWAR’s program manager for the SBIR/STTR programs, said about 60% of their entire SBIR and STTR awards take advantage of Catapult. The post NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kawasaki's CORLEO: A Hydrogen-Powered Robot for Off-Road Adventures
item.description: Kawasaki Heavy Industries has unveiled CORLEO, a hydrogen-powered, four-legged robot prototype designed to be ridden by humans. This futuristic machine is built to handle rugged terrain with ease, combining cutting-edge robotics and sustainable energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese Prime Minister Hopes Expo Will Unite World Amid Global Tensions
item.description: Osaka expo opens with hopes of global unity amid trade wars, conflicts, and divisions.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS employees consider workforce transition amid major uncertainty
item.description: Staff across DHS, including at CISA and FEMA, are considering deferred resignation or other offers amid reports that their agencies will be targeted for cuts. The post DHS employees consider workforce transition amid major uncertainty first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Social Security Administration 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Can't find your Android phone? Here's what to do to track it down
item.description: There are multiple ways to find your missing Android phone after it goes missing. One method involves using a smartwatch to ping your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation
